response:
In C# it is possible to measure the time taken for a UDP packet to reach a server  
but it is not straightforward  
UDP does not guarantee delivery or timing information  
To estimate the round trip time you can use the Ping class  
However this measures round trip time not one way  
To get one way time you would need to send a packet and receive a response  
This is not reliable with UDP  
You can use the Send and Receive methods of the UdpClient class  
Record the time before sending  
Then record the time when you receive a response  
The difference is the time taken  
This approach is not guaranteed to work  
It depends on whether the server responds  
You can also use the Timestamp option in the IP header  
This allows the system to record the time the packet was sent  
But this requires specific configuration  
It is not supported on all systems  
You can use the Timestamp and Timestamp戳 options  
These options are part of the IP header  
They allow the system to record the time the packet was sent  
You can then calculate the time difference  
This method is more accurate  
But it requires the server to support it  
In summary measuring one way UDP packet time is not easy  
It requires specific configuration and may not be reliable
